sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) Given Average Recommendation of "Hold" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from twenty-three brokerages, with an average 12-month price target of $181.33. Recent insider selling has been noted, with executives selling substantial shares, alongside mixed financial results for Q1 despite an earnings beat.

feed.title: Clear Channel Outdoor (NYSE:CCO) Shares Pass Above 200 Day Moving Average - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Clear Channel Outdoor (NYSE:CCO) shares recently surpassed their 200-day moving average, trading as high as $2.39. Despite this, analysts have a "Reduce" rating on the stock with an average price target of $2.29. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and institutional investors have been actively trading its shares.

feed.title: United Dominion Realty Trust, Inc. (NYSE:UDR) Receives Average Rating of "Hold" from Analysts
feed.summary: United Dominion Realty Trust, Inc. (NYSE:UDR) has received an average "Hold" rating from fifteen brokerages, with an average 12-month price target of $40.54. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.435, offering a 4.8% yield, and institutional investors hold a significant 97.82% of the stock. United Dominion Realty Trust, a real estate investment trust, specializes in multifamily apartment communities and engages in development, redevelopment, and strategic acquisitions.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences Corporation (NYSE:EW) Receives Average Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Brokerages

This follows strong Q1 earnings, where the company beat EPS and revenue estimates and provided optimistic FY2026 and Q2 guidance. Despite recent insider stock sales totaling $7.12 million, institutional investors hold a significant 79.46% of the company's stock.

feed.title: Simon Property Group, Inc. (NYSE:SPG) Given Consensus Recommendation of "Hold" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Simon Property Group (NYSE:SPG) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from brokerages, with ten out of fifteen firms recommending a "Hold" and five suggesting a "Buy." The average 12-month price target is set at $200.14. The company recently announced a $2.0 billion share buyback program and a quarterly dividend of $2.20, representing an annualized yield of approximately 4.3%.

feed.title: ResMed Inc. (NYSE:RMD) Given Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" by Brokerages

Despite recent insider sales totaling $5.23 million, the company reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst expectations with an 11% year-over-year revenue increase. Analysts project an EPS of $11.05 for the current fiscal year.

feed.title: Cognyte Software (NASDAQ:CGNT) Stock Crosses Above 200 Day Moving Average - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Cognyte Software (NASDAQ:CGNT) shares have recently crossed above their 200-day moving average, trading at $9.55 with a 200-day MA of $8.57. Several institutional investors have increased their stakes in the company.

feed.title: CMS Energy Corporation (NYSE:CMS) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
feed.summary: Analysts have issued a "Moderate Buy" consensus recommendation for CMS Energy Corporation (NYSE:CMS), with an average 12-month target price of $81.67. The company recently beat Q1 earnings estimates, reaffirmed its 2026 adjusted EPS guidance, and increased capital expenditure plans, signaling growth despite potential funding and execution risks. Insider transactions were mixed, and institutional holdings, notably by Vanguard, increased.

feed.title: Cummins Inc. (NYSE:CMI) Receives Average Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Brokerages
feed.summary: Seventeen analysts have given Cummins Inc. (NYSE:CMI) a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating, with an average 1-year price target of $600.27. The company recently surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $5.81 EPS versus $5.17 and $8.54 billion in revenue against $8.09 billion. Despite recent insider share sales, institutional ownership remains high at approximately 83.5%, and Cummins offers a $2.00 quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Maintains Overweight Rating on Constellation Energy (CEG)
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained an Overweight rating on Constellation Energy (CEG) but lowered its price target to $360 from $385 on April 21, 2026. This decision was part of a broader update for utilities and independent power producers, noting that utilities have outperformed the S&P in March. Concurrent with this, Evercore ISI resumed coverage of CEG with an Outperform rating and a $380 price target, following Constellation's acquisition of Calpine, which significantly expanded its clean energy capacity.

feed.title: Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:CHKP) Given Consensus Rating of "Hold" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:CHKP) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from twenty-three research firms, with an average one-year price target of $199.32. Analysts like Barclays and Truist have recently cut their price targets, despite the company beating quarterly EPS estimates ($3.40 vs. $2.77) and providing strong Q1 and FY 2026 guidance. Institutional investors hold approximately 98.51% of the shares, with the stock currently trading around $139.29.

feed.title: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO) Given Consensus Rating of "Hold" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Eighteen brokerages have given Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO) a consensus "Hold" rating, with an average 12-month target price of $84.27. This follows mixed Q1 results where revenue rose significantly, but organic revenue growth was flat and GAAP EPS declined, raising concerns despite the company's dividend declaration. Shares traded down approximately 4.7%, with institutional ownership at around 71%.

feed.title: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc. (NASDAQ:CBRL) Receives Consensus Rating of "Hold" from Brokerages
feed.summary: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc. (NASDAQ:CBRL) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from thirteen brokerages, with an average 1-year price target of $37.75. The company recently reported better-than-expected Q1 earnings per share of $0.25 against an estimated -$0.10, though revenue was down 7.9% year-over-year. CBRL also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.25, representing a 3.2% yield, but its payout ratio is negative due to recent losses.

feed.title: S&P Global Inc. (NYSE:SPGI) Given Consensus Recommendation of "Buy" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Nineteen brokerages have issued a "Buy" consensus recommendation for S&P Global Inc. (NYSE:SPGI), with an average 12-month price target of $549.13. The company recently reported strong Q1 results, beating analyst expectations on both EPS and revenue, though FY2026 EPS guidance caused some short-term volatility. Despite mixed analyst reactions and some caution regarding guidance, institutions own a significant portion of the stock.

feed.title: Is Akamai (AKAM) the Best Edge Computing Stock to Buy Right Now?
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AKAM) is highlighted as a leading edge computing stock, with recent increased price targets from Oppenheimer and Piper Sandler due to strong revenue expectations and adoption of its security modules and cloud infrastructure. The company also recently expanded its relationship with Arrow Electronics to enhance access to its cloud, security, and application delivery solutions. Despite the positive outlook for Akamai, the article suggests that other AI stocks might offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: CoStar Group Inc (CSGP) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Record
feed.summary: CoStar Group Inc (CSGP) announced record Q1 2026 results, including a 23% revenue increase to $897 million and a doubled adjusted EBITDA of $132 million. The company reported strong performance driven by both commercial and residential segments, with Homes.com showing significant returns despite challenges from an activist campaign and an inexperienced sales force. CoStar also outlined optimistic guidance for Q2 and the full year 2026, forecasting continued revenue growth and improved profitability, alongside strategic investments in platforms like Matterport and aggressive pricing adjustments for Homes.com.

feed.title: Did Citi's (C) AI-Powered Wealth Push and Debt Issuance Just Reframe Its Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Citigroup has recently completed several fixed-income offerings and launched significant AI-powered initiatives in its wealth management division, including Citi Sky, an AI assistant built with Google Cloud and Google DeepMind. These strategic moves aim to enhance client engagement and deepen relationships across various segments, aligning with Citi's broader transformation efforts. While these initiatives underscore the bank's commitment to technology and modernization, investors are still weighing them against ongoing regulatory and restructuring challenges to determine their true impact on Citi's investment narrative and future outlook.

feed.title: CMSI: Microsoft Corporation (MSFT.US) Updates Partnership with OpenAI; Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN.US) and Alphabet Benefit
feed.summary: CMSI reports that Microsoft (MSFT.US) has updated its partnership agreement with OpenAI, moving from an exclusive to a non-exclusive arrangement through 2032. This shift, partly driven by OpenAI's deal with Amazon, allows OpenAI multi-cloud flexibility while still committing substantial spending to Azure. The change benefits Amazon and Alphabet's cloud businesses, while Microsoft gains financial compensation and retains an equity stake, though it sacrifices exclusivity.

feed.title: UnitedHealth (UNH) Got Hammered. Q1 Shows the Comeback Has Started
feed.summary: UnitedHealth (UNH) reported decent Q1 earnings and raised its full-year earnings outlook, indicating a potential turnaround after regulatory pressures and inconsistent performance. The company's strategic decisions, including a favorable shift in the Medicare Advantage payment increase, aggressive AI investments, and a focus on the domestic market, are expected to drive long-term growth and margin expansion. Wall Street analysts currently give UNH an average price target of $384.59, suggesting a 4.86% upside, and the stock is considered attractively valued given the improving regulatory environment and operational efficiencies.

feed.title: Is It Time To Revisit Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) After Egg Market Headlines?
feed.summary: This article examines Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) after recent egg market headlines, assessing its current valuation using two methods. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 31.2%, with an intrinsic value of $112.31 per share compared to its current price of approximately $77. However, comparing Cal-Maine Foods' P/E ratio of 5.3x to its proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 3.3x indicates the stock might be overvalued on that metric.

feed.title: Is It Time To Revisit IQVIA (IQV) After Its Sharp Year To Date Share Price Drop
feed.summary: IQVIA Holdings (IQV) has experienced a significant year-to-date share price drop of 29.5%, leading to questions about its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is currently undervalued by 49.1% with an estimated intrinsic value of US$312.09 per share compared to its recent close of US$158.98. Additionally, its P/E ratio of 19.62x is below the industry average and Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio," further indicating potential undervaluation.

feed.title: John Deere delivers reliability for Mountford Berries
feed.summary: Mountford Berries, a Tasmanian farm, has adopted a John Deere 5105ML Specialty Tractor to improve efficiency in its berry-growing operations. The tractor's narrow design allows it to navigate tight polytunnels without damaging crops, while its advanced software offers streamlined record-keeping and enhanced traceability. This investment helps the farm manage the rising costs and labor-intensive nature of hydroponic berry production.

feed.title: Does Expanded MTAP-CDx Partnership With Foundation Medicine Change The Bull Case For Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY)?
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb has expanded its partnership with Foundation Medicine to develop a companion diagnostic for an investigational targeted cancer therapy, deepening its push into precision oncology. This collaboration could enhance the company's long-term investment narrative by reinforcing its focus on biomarker-driven cancer care, but it does not immediately mitigate near-term patent-cliff risks for key drugs like Eliquis and Opdivo. The market is watching how this and other pipeline developments will influence Bristol-Myers Squibb's future revenue and earnings, especially given existing analyst expectations.

feed.title: Fortinet Report Reveals Cybersecurity Hiring Stalls as Nearly Half of IT Leaders Face Corporate Pushback
feed.summary: A new report from Fortinet highlights persistent cybersecurity skill shortages, with many organizations experiencing security breaches and struggling to get approval for new cybersecurity talent. Despite the challenges, there's growing investment in certifications and AI-powered cybersecurity tools, though AI also presents new risks and demands for specialized skills. Fortinet emphasizes the need for a layered approach to cybersecurity, combining people, processes, and technology, and offers training programs to address the skills gap.

feed.title: PACCAR Inc (PCAR) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Finan
feed.summary: PACCAR Inc (PCAR) reported robust first-quarter 2026 financial results with $6.8 billion in revenue and $605 million in net income, despite ongoing market challenges. The company is making strategic investments in capital and R&D for future growth, including electric vehicle offerings and advanced manufacturing. While facing soft parts markets, competitive pricing, and raw material pressures, PACCAR anticipates performance improvements and margin expansion in the second half of the year as global production volumes increase.

feed.title: Incyte Corp (INCY) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Reve
feed.summary: Incyte Corp (INCY) reported a strong start to 2026 with a 21% increase in total revenue, reaching $1.27 billion, and an 18% rise in R&D expenses as it advances its late-stage pipeline. The company is strategically diversifying its portfolio beyond its cornerstone product, Jakafi, with promising developments in povorcitinib for hidradenitis suppurativa and nonsegmental vitiligo, and other oncology and immunology trials. Despite challenges in market competition and sustained reliance on pipeline success, Incyte maintains a positive outlook with increased full-year sales guidance for its key products.

feed.title: Xylem Inc (XYL) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Resilient Perf
feed.summary: Xylem Inc (XYL) reported resilient Q1 2026 financial performance, including solid EPS growth and increased dividend, despite flat revenue attributed to challenges in China and strategic shifts. The company made a significant acquisition and booked its largest-ever outsourced water contract, highlighting strong demand in key segments. Xylem maintains a positive outlook with increased full-year revenue and EBITDA margin guidance, driven by smart metering demand and robust infrastructure needs.

feed.title: Cognex (CGNX) Projected to Post Quarterly Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: Cognex (NASDAQ:CGNX) is expected to announce its Q1 2026 earnings on Wednesday, May 6th, with analysts projecting earnings of $0.25 per share and revenue of $245.576 million. The company previously exceeded estimates in Q4, reporting $0.27 EPS and $252.34 million in revenue. Despite recent insider selling, institutional ownership remains high, and several analyst firms have raised their price targets for CGNX.

feed.title: Vanguard (NYSE: ARW) reports 3.22M-share, 6.3% stake in Arrow Electronics
feed.summary: Vanguard Portfolio Management has reported a beneficial ownership of 3,222,548 shares, representing a 6.3% stake in Arrow Electronics (ARW), as disclosed in a Schedule 13G SEC filing. This filing indicates a passive investment intent, with Vanguard exercising sole dispositive power over all reported shares but sole voting power for only 24,046 shares. The holdings are distributed across various Vanguard funds and managed accounts.

feed.title: AstraZeneca beats profit expectations, keeps annual outlook unchanged
feed.summary: AstraZeneca surpassed first-quarter sales and profit estimates, largely due to strong demand for its cancer drugs. The company reaffirmed its 2026 financial outlook, projecting low double-digit percentage growth in core earnings per share and mid-to-high single-digit revenue growth at constant currencies. CEO Pascal Soriot continues to lead the company toward its goal of $80 billion in annual sales by 2030, expanding its global presence despite geopolitical complexities.

feed.title: PTC (PTC) Expected to Announce Quarterly Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: PTC (NASDAQ:PTC) is scheduled to release its Q2 2026 earnings on Wednesday, May 6th, with analysts forecasting earnings of $2.11 per share and revenue of $715.45 million. The company exceeded expectations in its previous quarter, reporting $1.92 EPS against an anticipated $1.59, and its revenue increased 21.4% year-over-year. Wall Street maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $182.08 for PTC.

feed.title: MetLife (MET) Projected to Post Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: MetLife (MET) is expected to announce its Q1 2026 earnings after market close on Wednesday, May 6th, with analysts projecting $2.20 EPS and $19.29 billion in revenue. The company previously beat estimates in Q4 2025 with $2.49 EPS and $52.44 billion in revenue. Currently, the stock has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and a target price of $93.38.

feed.title: China suspends new autonomous vehicle permits after Baidu outage, Bloomberg News reports
feed.summary: China has reportedly suspended the issuance of new permits for autonomous vehicles after an outage affected Baidu's robotaxis in Wuhan last month. This halt prevents self-driving companies from expanding their fleets, launching new pilot projects, or entering new cities. Baidu's robotaxi operations in Wuhan are also suspended pending an investigation.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley launches first major Wall Street spot Bitcoin ETF
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has launched its spot Bitcoin ETF (MSBT), marking it as the first major Wall Street bank to offer such a product. The MSBT carries a 0.14% fee, aiming to attract institutional capital, but the market's expectation for Bitcoin reaching $80,000 in April has dropped, indicating that traders believe broader headwinds beyond the ETF's launch are influencing Bitcoin's price. The article suggests that future net inflow data for MSBT and regulatory shifts will be key factors to watch for Bitcoin's price movements.

feed.title: Colgate, in partnership with Hong Kong Dental Association, launches TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab
feed.summary: Colgate, in partnership with the Hong Kong Dental Association, has launched the "TOTAL Periodontal Health Lab" to raise public awareness about gum disease prevention. The initiative, supported by PARKnSHOP and Watsons, includes Hong Kong's first community-wide oral health survey aiming to collect 5,000 data samples to inform future oral care strategies and offers free dental consultations and interactive educational activities. It extends Colgate's "Bright Smiles, Bright Futures" mission by donating toothpaste for every smile collected for charity.

feed.title: Vanguard Portfolio Management (AROC) discloses 6.33% ownership, 11.1M shares
feed.summary: Vanguard Portfolio Management has disclosed a 6.33% ownership stake in Archrock Inc. (AROC), amounting to 11,095,912 shares of common stock as of March 31, 2026. The filing, a Schedule 13G, indicates that Vanguard holds sole dispositive power over all these shares but only sole voting power for 158,065 shares, signifying a passive investment. This aggregated holding reflects securities managed across Vanguard Portfolio Management LLC and its affiliates.

feed.title: Black Hills (BKH) Announces Quarterly Dividend of $0.703
feed.summary: Black Hills Corporation (BKH) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.703 per share, maintaining its previous rate, which offers a forward yield of 3.74%. The company's GF Score™ of 76/100 suggests strong potential for long-term returns, balanced against a lower financial strength rating. While profitability is solid, concerns exist regarding declining revenue per share and a P/E ratio near historical highs.

feed.title: American Eagle (NYSE: AEO) awards 915 dividend rights to brand president
feed.summary: American Eagle (NYSE: AEO) Global Brand President, Jennifer M. Foyle, was granted 915 dividend equivalent rights tied to previously awarded restricted stock units (RSUs). This grant increases her total reported dividend equivalent rights to 3,666. These rights are the economic equivalent of one share of common stock per right and will vest proportionately with the related RSUs.

feed.title: DRONE STRATEGY: AEROVIRONMENT, VOLATUS AEROSPACE, OR DRONESHIELD – WHICH ONE WILL TAKE OFF AGAIN?
feed.summary: This article analyzes the investment prospects of three drone-related companies: AeroVironment, Volatus Aerospace, and DroneShield, amidst a cooling market for drone stocks. It examines their respective business models—offensive drones, versatile ISR and logistics drones, and counter-drone defense systems—and their financial valuations. The author questions which company is best positioned for future growth, especially given current high valuations and the shift from euphoria to a more discerning market.

feed.title: Stryker (SYK) Q1 Earnings: What To Expect
feed.summary: Stryker (NYSE:SYK) is anticipated to report its Q1 earnings this Thursday, with market expectations for an 8% year-on-year revenue growth. The company has a history of exceeding Wall Street's expectations and analysts have reconfirmed their estimates. Peers in the medical devices & supplies segment have shown mixed results, while investor sentiment in the sector has been positive overall.

feed.title: Gold Shrugs Off the Oil Crisis – Who Are the Winners? Barrick Mining, DRC Gold, and Occidental Petroleum in the Spotlight
feed.summary: Despite escalating Middle East conflicts and rising gas prices, gold remains stable, highlighting its role as a crisis hedge. The article discusses Barrick Mining's diversification across gold and copper, DRC Gold's promising East African gold projects, and Occidental Petroleum’s unexpected gains from oil market volatility. It highlights these companies as potential winners in the current volatile economic climate, suggesting they offer significant investment opportunities.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Axos Financial (AX) After Its Strong 1-Year Share Price Rally?
feed.summary: Axos Financial (AX) has seen a significant 54.7% share price rally over the last year, prompting questions about its current valuation. According to Simply Wall St's Excess Returns model, the stock appears significantly undervalued by 62.6%, with an intrinsic value estimate of $264.41 per share compared to its current trading price of $98.89. The company's P/E ratio of 12.27x also suggests it is undervalued when compared to Simply Wall St's proprietary "Fair Ratio" of 14.87x.

feed.title: AvalonBay Communities Q1 FFO Softness Tests Bullish Narrative On Long Term Apartment Demand
feed.summary: AvalonBay Communities (AVB) reported Q1 2026 revenue of US$770.1 million and FFO of US$383.6 million, a slight decrease from Q1 2025. The company's net margin subtly declined, and a significant one-off gain of US$460.8 million influenced reported earnings, masking an underlying forecast decline of 11.1% per year for the next three years. Despite trading below its industry average P/E and having a substantial DCF fair value, the mixed financial signals and debt coverage issues present a complex picture for investors.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reassess AdaptHealth (AHCO) After A 60% One Year Rally?
feed.summary: AdaptHealth (AHCO) has seen a significant 60% rally over the past year, prompting a reassessment of its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 64.0%, while its P/S ratio indicates a modest discount compared to a proprietary fair ratio. The article also presents bullish and bearish narratives, with fair values ranging from US$9.50 to US$17.00 per share, offering investors different perspectives on the company's future.

feed.title: NRG Energy Debt Tender Reshapes Covenants And Investor Risk Profile
feed.summary: NRG Energy's subsidiary, Lightning Power, has successfully completed a cash offer for its 7.250% senior secured notes, receiving over 99% early tenders. This move includes securing consent to amend the notes indenture, thereby removing restrictive covenants and releasing certain guarantees and collateral. This strategic debt restructuring aims to adjust NRG's balance sheet and future obligations in a single transaction, influencing its financial flexibility and interest costs going forward.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) Sees Q1 FFO Surge Questioning Bearish Cash Flow Narratives
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) reported a significant surge in Q1 2026 funds from operations (FFO) to US$636.9 million, despite trailing 12-month net income losses of US$1.1 billion. This strong FFO performance supports the bullish argument that the company's long-duration life science leases and large campus investments can generate substantial cash flow even amid accounting pressures and declining revenue. However, the stock trades below analyst targets and its DCF fair value, reflecting investor concerns over persistent losses and interest expenses.

feed.title: Trash and the Hunger for Power: How Waste Management, Zefiro Methane, and NextEra Energy Are Doing Good - and Making Money
feed.summary: This article examines how Waste Management, Zefiro Methane, and NextEra Energy are tackling environmental challenges while profitably expanding their businesses. Waste Management is diversifying into medical waste disposal, Zefiro Methane is addressing abandoned oil and gas wells, and NextEra Energy is meeting the high energy demands of the AI industry with sustainable solutions. These companies demonstrate how economic activity can align with environmental responsibility, providing stable and growth-oriented investment opportunities.

feed.title: Akebia (NASDAQ: AKBA) plans major increase in authorized common shares
feed.summary: Akebia Therapeutics is seeking shareholder approval to significantly increase its authorized common shares from 350 million to 500 million, and total capital stock from 375 million to 525 million, at its virtual 2026 annual meeting on June 17, 2026. This move aims to provide the company with greater flexibility for future financing, strategic deals, and equity incentives without requiring further charter amendments. Shareholders will also vote on director elections, executive compensation, its frequency, and the ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor.

feed.title: NetApp collabs with Google Cloud for AI-driven operations
feed.summary: NetApp is deepening its collaboration with Google Cloud by adopting Google Cloud AI technologies and Gemini Enterprise internally. This move aims to leverage AI for enhanced productivity in product development and sales operations, while also helping NetApp's customers integrate AI with their data. The partnership emphasizes delivering AI-driven insights to accelerate development cycles and provide more relevant solutions to customers, with NetApp acting as an "AI practitioner."

feed.title: Addex GABAB PAM Candidate Demonstrates Solid Anti-Tussive Activity in Bleomycin IPF-Related Chronic Cough Model
feed.summary: Addex Therapeutics announced promising preclinical data for its GABAB positive allosteric modulator (PAM) candidate, showing significant anti-tussive activity in a bleomycin-induced idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) exacerbated chronic cough model. The compound demonstrated robust and sustained reductions in cough frequency, improved lung pathology, and maintained a favorable safety profile. This supports its potential as a novel and well-tolerated treatment for chronic cough, addressing a significant unmet medical need, particularly in IPF patients where current treatments like baclofen have limiting side effects.
